Methods

How the pipeline works under the hood

1
Literature Fetching

7 epilepsy-specific search terms queried against PubMed via Biopython's Entrez API. Results filtered to last 10 years, deduplicated by PMID, and quality-filtered to abstracts ≥150 words.

2
Claim Extraction

Each abstract sent to Claude claude-sonnet-4-20250514 with a structured prompt requesting mechanistic claims in JSON. Each claim captures subject, direction, effect, experimental condition, and confidence level.

3
Contradiction Detection

Rule-based pre-filter: claims are paired if they share a subject (fuzzy substring match), have opposing directions (increases↔decreases, activates↔inhibits), and overlapping effect descriptions. Top candidates scored and re-evaluated by Claude as true/conditional/terminology/not-contradictory.

Claim Schema 
{
  "subject":   "sodium channel Nav1.1",
  "direction": "decreases",
  "effect":    "action potential threshold",
  "condition": {
    "model":         "mouse",
    "cell_type":     "GABAergic interneuron",
    "epilepsy_type": "Dravet syndrome"
  },
  "confidence": "primary finding",
  "method":    ["patch-clamp", "AAV injection"]
}
⚠ Limitations
Abstract-only analysis (full text not accessed) · 2025–2026 publication window (limited accumulation of contradictions) · Fuzzy subject matching may miss synonyms (e.g. Nav1.1 vs SCN1A) · LLM extraction errors possible, especially for complex multi-part claims.

EpiContradiction — Cathy Liu · Built with PubMed API + Claude Sonnet 4.5
